hypothesis: Systematic biases in GFN2-xTB geometry optimization failures are non-random with respect to molecular topology—specifically, molecules containing strained ring systems, highly conjugated heterocycles, or metal-coordinating functional groups will exhibit disproportionately higher fragmentation rates during GFN2-xTB optimization, such that removing these fractured structures from training data introduces a measurable topological bias in learned generative model distributions that can be detected as statistically significant underrepresentation of those substructure classes in generated molecules compared to the full unfiltered GEOM-Drugs dataset.
